name: objective scoring: the low-cost option scores higher on cost-effectiveness than the high-cost option
command:
cat > opts.json <<'JEOF'
{"option_count":4,"options":[{"option_id":"opt-cost","summary":"Cost-optimized managed baseline","trade_offs":{"cost":"low","complexity":"low","performance":"medium","availability":"medium","vendor_lock_in":"medium"}},{"option_id":"opt-balanced","summary":"Balanced hybrid","trade_offs":{"cost":"medium","complexity":"medium","performance":"medium","availability":"high","vendor_lock_in":"low"}},{"option_id":"opt-resilient","summary":"Resilient scale-out","trade_offs":{"cost":"high","complexity":"high","performance":"high","availability":"high","vendor_lock_in":"medium"}},{"option_id":"opt-max","summary":"Maximum-resilience multi-region","trade_offs":{"cost":"high","complexity":"high","performance":"high","availability":"very-high","vendor_lock_in":"medium"}}]}
JEOF
bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/optimize-options.sh" --options opts.json --out sc.json --now 2026-06-08T12:00:00Z 2>/dev/null
node -e 'const j=JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync("sc.json","utf8"));const c=j.scored_options.find(o=>o.option_id==="opt-cost").scores.cost_effective;const m=j.scored_options.find(o=>o.option_id==="opt-max").scores.cost_effective;process.exit(c>m?0:1)' && echo cost-score 1>&2
